text:
In the heart of the enchanted forest, there lived a wise old owl. Every evening, after scanning the moon-rimed canopy for signs of danger, he would descend into his cozy nest high on a towering branch.

One autumn night, as he fluttered away to bed, singing ancient lullabies, a tiny fox trotted over to his nest. The fox was enchanted.

"Hello," said the fox.

"No one ever talks to me here," mumbled the owl, half-asleep.

"I didn't know you were a talker."

"I could ask you the same thing."

The fox thought for a moment. "How about a story? Every night I listen to your stories."

"A story?" the owl wondered.

"Yes. Would you tell me tales? I wander these woods at night."

"Well," the owl pondered. "There was once a brave forest mouse who dreamed of becoming an admiral."

The fox sat up, captivated.

"Every night when I'm awake,"
